commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=044d5092b8f2e823d0820bbffedbb7644cf7fc97)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an Anthropic long-context usage error classifier, exports it from the agent helper barrel, and adds focused classifier tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at the classifier level: source inspection shows the target string is already a billing match and the PR's new overflow branch runs after that early return. I did not reproduce a live Anthropic 429 session.
commands.review_followup: This needs maintainer provider-routing judgment and contributor-supplied real behavior proof; ClawSweeper repair cannot provide the contributor's Anthropic setup evidence.

target.title: fix(agent): treat Anthropic long-context usage errors as context overflow for compact+retry
target.body:
## Summary

Re-opening as new PR after #52030 was auto-closed by barnaclebot for the 10-PR cap (March 2026); reviewers and Greptile evaluated the fix positively at the time and the underlying bug still applies on current main.

Anthropic returns HTTP 429 with body `"Extra usage is required for long context requests."` when a request hits the long-context usage tier. Today the runtime treats this as a transient rate limit and runs it through the model fallback chain, but it is semantically a **context overflow** — the session is too large for the standard usage tier — and should route to **compact + retry** instead.

This PR adds `isAnthropicLongContextUsageError(message)` and teaches `isLikelyContextOverflowError(message)` to short-circuit to `true` for that exact body, before the rate-limit exclusion runs.

## Files

- `src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/errors.ts`: new classifier + early-return in the overflow check
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.ts`: re-export
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts`: unit coverage for the new classifier and the overflow short-circuit

## Verification

**Behavior addressed:** Anthropic's `Extra usage is required for long context requests.` 429 was misclassified as a transient rate-limit, sending sessions into the fallback model chain (wrong) instead of compact+retry (right).

**Real environment tested:** Yes — verified locally against the new classifier tests and the overflow check.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
\`\`\`
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts
\`\`\`

**Evidence after fix:**

\`\`\`
RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/alex/git/openclaw

src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts (passes both the new isAnthropicLongContextUsageError suite and the extended isLikelyContextOverflowError case)
\`\`\`

**Observed result after fix:**
- `isAnthropicLongContextUsageError("Extra usage is required for long context requests.")` -> `true`
- `isLikelyContextOverflowError("Extra usage is required for long context requests.")` -> `true` (previously `false`, routed to rate-limit fallback)
- Standard rate-limit messages (`"Rate limit exceeded"`, `"429 Too Many Requests"`) still return `false` from the new classifier and continue to route through the rate-limit path.

**What was not tested:** Live end-to-end against a real Anthropic long-context 429 response (would require a session above the standard usage tier).

## Refs

- Superseded close: #52030 (barnaclebot 10-PR cap auto-close, no merit rejection)
- Original Greptile review on #52030 confirmed the misclassification path
